title: When Judging Groups: - Challenge Assumptions: Ask,
subtitle: See the Individual in the Group
description:
When judging groups:
- Challenge assumptions: Ask, "Does this apply to all members, or just one person?"
- Seek evidence: Look for facts about the group, not just individual actions.
- Avoid overgeneralizing: Treat group decisions as collective, not necessarily representative of everyone.

Example: If one team member misses a deadline, don’t assume the entire team is unreliable.
keywords: See the Individual in the Group

howTo.title: Pause and Reflect
howTo.summary: When you hear a negative group judgment, take a 30-second pause. Ask yourself if the statement applies to the entire group or just one individual.

howTo.title: Gather Evidence
howTo.summary: Collect data to support or refute the group claim. Look for independent sources and counterexamples to ensure a fair assessment.

howTo.title: Use the Diagnostic Questions
howTo.summary: In conversations, apply the diagnostic question set to clarify who is being discussed and what evidence supports the claim. This promotes accurate judgments.

howTo.title: Implement a Check-in Routine
howTo.summary: Establish a habit of checking in on group judgments regularly. Encourage team members to ask clarifying questions before making assumptions.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce the habit. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/judge-groups-fairly/.
